treatment_information.dosage_or_details: repeat cesarean at 28 weeks of gestation
treatment_information.treatment_response: delivery of a 1014 g baby with Apgars 5/7

treatment_information.dosage_or_details: surgical repair due to feeding problems

treatment_timeline.event: referred for targeted sonography due to abnormal midtrimester ultrasound showing cystic formations within the placenta
genetic amniocentesis showed normal female karyotype
delivery by repeat cesarean due to severe preeclampsia
placental cytogenetic studies showed a triploid placenta (69,XXX)
neonate’s peripheral blood karyotype was normal
diagnosis of ileal stricture, requiring surgical repair

patient_outcome.final_status: At 6 years, weight and neurodevelopment reported normal
patient_outcome.complications: feeding problems leading to ileal stricture, bilateral inguinal hernias
